description:
You are an actuary tasked with forecasting French mortality to update pension longevity assumptions.

Using RStudio, write a script to model and forecast French mortality rates using the Lee-Carter method.

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Install and load the 'demography' and 'forecast' packages.
2. Load the built-in 'fr.mort' dataset (French mortality rates, 1816-2006) from the demography package.
3. Subset the data to use ONLY the years 1960-2006 to avoid wartime distortions (use the extract.years function).
4. Fit a Lee-Carter model (lca function) to this subset.
5. Forecast the mortality index (k_t) parameter for the next 30 years (2007-2036).
6. Extract the forecasted life expectancy at birth (e0) for these 30 years.
7. Save the projected life expectancy to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/mortality_forecast_e0.csv' with columns representing Year and e0.
8. Save the Lee-Carter age-specific parameters (a_x and b_x) to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/leecarter_parameters.csv' with columns for Age, ax, and bx.
9. Save a plot of the historical and forecasted k_t trend (with prediction intervals) to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/kt_trend_plot.png'.

RStudio is open. Write and execute your code in the provided /home/ga/RProjects/mortality_forecast.R script.
